mnemonics: PAIR: Probability And Its Rules. Think of pairing events together to understand their likelihood.
keyPoints: Probability (P) is a number between 0 and 1 that quantifies uncertainty.
An event is a set of outcomes from a sample space (e.g., rolling an even number on a die).
The probability of an event A is written as P(A), representing its chance of occurring.
commonMistakes: Confusing P(A|B) (probability of A given B) with P(A and B) (joint probability of A and B).
Assuming all events are independent when they are not, leading to incorrect probability calculations.
